title: Adversarial Thinking Warm-Up
tools_required: Pen and paper or text editor
when_to_use: Before any vulnerability assessment or when developing adversarial thinking skills

steps.title: Select a Target App
steps.instruction: Pick any app on your phone or browser
steps.tip: Choose something you use daily — familiarity helps you think deeper about assumptions
steps.expected_output: A specific app selected for analysis

steps.title: List Assumptions
steps.instruction: List 5 assumptions it makes about users (e.g., users will only enter valid data)
steps.tip: Think about authentication, payment, input handling, session management, and data storage

steps.title: Violate Each Assumption
steps.instruction: For each assumption, ask: what if someone intentionally violated this?
steps.tip: Think like a malicious user with technical knowledge — not just accidental misuse

steps.title: Assess Consequences
steps.instruction: Write the potential consequences of each violation
steps.tip: Consider data loss, financial impact, reputation damage, and cascading effects

steps.title: Rank by Severity
steps.instruction: Rank by severity: you have just completed a basic threat model
steps.tip: Use a simple High/Medium/Low ranking. The highest severity items are your priority findings
